response.justification: The candidate is an experienced engineer (13+ years) with strong fundamentals in testable design, high-performance/low-latency systems, and concurrent programming (async I/O, fibers, microservices). However, this role is C++-centric, and the candidate's C++ exposure is minimal and dated — roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a bachelor thesis — and C++ is not even listed in their skills section. Their core stack is JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of message brokers, MQTT, IEC 62443/SDL, industrial automation, or Azure, all of which are central or advantageous to this position. The mismatch on the primary technical requirement outweighs the otherwise solid generalist profile.
strengths: Strong concurrent/high-performance background (async I/O, fibers, low-latency microservices at scale)
Long, broad engineering career (13+ years) with embedded, backend, and craftsmanship/TDD practices
Demonstrated mentoring and documentation ability (workshops, courses, talks)
concerns: Minimal and dated C++ experience (~6 months); C++ not listed among core skills
No evidence of message brokers, MQTT/REST IoT, or industrial automation domain
No secure development lifecycle / IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity experience
